skill.name: frontend-nextjs-app-router
skill.description: Guides Next.js App Router development for pages, dynamic routes, layouts, Server and Client Components, route handlers, and role-based dashboards.
skill.summary: Build and review Next.js App Router pages, layouts, routes, and dashboard patterns.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe patterns, but review found they are almost entirely Markdown and TypeScript documentation false positives. No malicious intent, prompt injection, executable scripts, credential collection, or data exfiltration was found. The skill has medium risk because several examples can encourage under-validated server actions, route handlers, and route protection patterns.

medium_findings.title: Examples Omit Input Validation For Server-Side Mutations
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE semantic concern. Server Action and route handler examples read form or request body data and pass it to database helpers without showing validation, authorization, or error handling. The snippets are documentation, not active code, but they may lead generated applications to accept unsafe input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly show unvalidated mutation inputs in server-side contexts. Confidence is not higher because the code is illustrative documentation and helper functions may validate internally.

medium_findings.title: Simplified Route Protection Examples May Encourage Weak Auth
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE semantic concern. The middleware example only checks whether a token cookie exists before allowing protected routes. The admin page example checks a session role but does not show session integrity validation, creating a risk that users copy incomplete authorization patterns.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The snippets are demonstrably incomplete for production authorization. Confidence is limited because they may be abbreviated examples rather than a full auth implementation.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The many reported Ruby or shell backtick findings come from Markdown code fences, inline route paths, JSX template literals, and TypeScript examples. I found no instruction that executes shell commands or asks the assistant to run untrusted commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation syntax and TypeScript examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. The skill contains only Markdown files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text-Match False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The reported weak cryptographic algorithm locations contain ordinary words such as description or design-related prose, not MD5, SHA-1, DES, or cryptographic API usage. No evidence found of weak cryptography instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found no cryptographic algorithm references at the flagged locations. The matches appear to be substring-based analyzer noise.

low_findings.title: Network, Storage, And Path Traversal Findings Are Benign Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The fetch, localStorage, and /app/.../page.tsx references describe normal Next.js and browser concepts. They do not collect credentials, access files, traverse paths, or make external network requests from the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged tokens are explanatory framework terms inside Markdown. No executable file access, browser storage access, or outbound request code exists in the skill package.

low_findings.title: Critical Heuristic Combinations Are Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The static heuristic combined unrelated documentation terms and examples into a dangerous pattern. Review found no credential access, exfiltration endpoint, obfuscation, executable script, or prompt injection attempt.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the referenced files showed only instructional Markdown and short Next.js examples. The dangerous combination is not semantically present.

content.value_statement: Next.js App Router work often needs correct routing, layout, and component boundaries. This skill gives targeted guidance for building pages, dynamic routes, dashboards, metadata, loading states, and route handlers.
actual_capabilities: Creates App Router page, layout, template, loading, error, and not-found file patterns.
Explains dynamic routes, route groups, parallel routes, and intercepting routes.
Guides Server Component and Client Component separation for data and interactivity.
Provides dashboard and role-based page patterns for admin, teacher, and student views.
Shows metadata, streaming, Suspense, image optimization, and revalidation examples.
Includes shadcn/ui and Recharts usage patterns for common frontend screens.
limitations: Examples are templates and may need validation, authorization, and error handling before production use.
The skill focuses on App Router frontend structure, not full backend architecture.
It does not install packages or run project commands by itself.
Framework details may need updates for future Next.js releases.

use_cases.title: Create Product Pages
use_cases.description: Build App Router pages with metadata, loading states, and dynamic route parameters.

use_cases.title: Organize Dashboard Routes
use_cases.description: Design nested layouts, route groups, and role-specific dashboard screens for an education or ERP app.

use_cases.title: Review Component Boundaries
use_cases.description: Decide when to use Server Components, Client Components, Suspense, and client-side chart libraries.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Next.js App Router page for a dashboard. Use a Server Component by default, add metadata, and include a loading state if data is fetched.

prompt_templates.title: Add A Dynamic Route
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an App Router dynamic route for student profiles. Include params handling, generateMetadata, a not-found state, and a clear folder structure.

prompt_templates.title: Design Role Dashboards
prompt_templates.prompt: Design admin, teacher, and student dashboard routes using layouts, route groups, Server Components, and Client Components where needed.

prompt_templates.title: Audit An App Router Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this App Router implementation for routing correctness, component boundaries, metadata, loading states, accessibility, and missing validation or authorization.

output_examples.input: Create a course detail route for /courses/[courseId].
output_examples.output: The result would describe the app/courses/[courseId]/page.tsx structure, metadata generation, parameter handling, not-found behavior, and data loading responsibilities.

output_examples.input: Add analytics charts to an admin dashboard.
output_examples.output: The result would separate server-side data fetching from a client chart component and explain where layout, loading, and chart files belong.
The result would identify routing issues, missing layouts, incorrect client boundaries, and production checks such as auth, validation, and accessibility.

best_practices: Use Server Components by default and add Client Components only for browser APIs, hooks, or interaction.
Pair dynamic routes with metadata, not-found handling, and explicit parameter validation.
Treat example auth and mutation snippets as starting points that need production security checks.
anti_patterns: Putting use client at the top of large route trees without a specific interaction need.
Copying route handlers or Server Actions without validating input and checking authorization.
Using nested layouts, templates, or parallel routes when a simple page structure is enough.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it generate complete Next.js applications?
faq.answer: No. It provides App Router patterns and implementation guidance for specific features.

faq.question: Can it help with dynamic routes?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers params, generateMetadata, not-found handling, and folder naming for dynamic routes.

faq.question: Does it cover Server Components?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains Server Component defaults and when Client Components are needed.

faq.question: Are the examples production ready?
faq.answer: Not always. Add validation, authorization, logging, and error handling before using mutation or auth examples in production.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands or access external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed package contains Markdown guidance and examples, not executable scripts.
